author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
e mana! ki lāgi’ āili bhave
emana janame, ‘hari’ nā bhajili,
se tui mānuṣa kabe

mānuṣa⋅ākāra, hoile ki haya,
karaha bhūtera kāma
nahile vadane, kena nā balaha,
‘śrī⋅kṛṣṇa’ ‘govinda’⋅nāma

pākhīre je⋅nāma, looyāile laya,
śārī śuka ādi kata
tumi je ihāte, ālasya karaha,
e haya kemana mata

divasa⋅rajanī, ābola tābola,
pacāla pāḍite pāra
tāhāra bhitare, kakhana kena ki,
‘govinda’ balite nāra

bhajiba baliye, kahiyā āili,
bhulili ki sukha pāiye
bujhinu ābāra, śamana⋅nagare,
narake majibi jāiye

vadana bhariyā, ‘hari’ bala jadi,
kṣati nā hoibe tāya
kahe premānanda, tabe je nitānta,
eḍābe kṛtānta⋅dāya
en_translation:
O my mind! For what purpose did you come to this material world? In such a rare human birth, you have not worshipped Lord Hari. When will you truly act as a human being?

What is the use of having a human form if you perform only the acts of a ghost? Otherwise, why do you not utter the Names of Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Govinda with your mouth?

Even birds like parrots and mynahs will chant the Name if taught. Yet you are lazy in this matter—how can this be appropriate?

Day and night you can speak nonsense and engage in idle chatter. In the midst of all that, why can you not utter the Name of Lord Govinda?

You came to this world promising to worship the Lord, but having forgotten that, what happiness have you found? I understand that you will eventually drown in hell in the city of the God of Death.

If you wholeheartedly chant the Name of Lord Hari, it will do you no harm. Premānanda says: "Only then will you certainly escape the grasp of the God of Death."
